1210	  \emph{First reduction (locality on \(S'\)).} By
1211	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} it suffices to prove that the
1212	  base-change map of Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an
1213	  isomorphism on sections over every affine open of \(S'\). This is exactly the
1214	  locality reduction Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}: restricting the
1215	  cartesian square to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R') \subseteq S'\) and
1216	  a chosen affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing \(g(U)\), the
1217	  hypothesis \([\mathrm{IsAffineHom}\ f]\) makes \(X\) restrict to
1218	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) makes it restrict
1219	  to \(\widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\). (It is here that quasi-coherence is
1220	  used: it is what licenses writing \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\).) Computing the
1221	  fibre product gives \(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\) and
1222	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) the tilde of the \((R' \otimes_R A)\)-module
1223	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\). The matter is thus reduced to the affine--affine
1224	  model, where the base-change map is a morphism \(\alpha\) of quasi-coherent
1225	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R')\)-modules.
1226	
1227	  \medskip
1228	  \emph{Passage to global sections.} The functor
1229	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \operatorname{ModuleCat}(R') \to
1230	  (\operatorname{Spec} R').\mathrm{Modules}\) is fully faithful with essential image
1231	  the quasi-coherent modules; equivalently the counit
1232	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{N})} \to \mathcal{N}\) is an isomorphism exactly when
1233	  \(\mathcal{N}\) is quasi-coherent. Source and target of \(\alpha\) are
1234	  quasi-coherent, so both counits are isomorphisms and, by their naturality,
1235	  \[
1236	    \mathrm{IsIso}\,\alpha
1237	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
1238	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl(\Gamma(\alpha)\bigr),
1239	  \]
1240	  where \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is a concrete \(R'\)-linear map in
1241	  \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\). The whole computation therefore stays at the level
1242	  of modules.
1243	
1244	  \medskip
1245	  \emph{Section-level identification (the second obligation).} By the section-level
1246	  computation Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}, the map
1247	  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\), with its domain \(\Gamma(g^*(f_*\widetilde{M}))\) identified with
1248	  \(R' \otimes_R M\) and its codomain \(\Gamma(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde{M})\) identified
1249	  with \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) through the two dictionaries
1250	  Lemmas~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} and~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso},
1251	  \emph{is} the canonical cancellation isomorphism
1252	  \[
1253	    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} :
1254	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M,
1255	    \qquad (r' \otimes a) \otimes m \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m)
1256	  \]
1257	  of \(R'\)-modules (Mathlib's
1258	  \texttt{TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange}). This is the entire
1259	  content of the affine close, and it is performed \emph{directly on the module of
1260	  global sections} — there is no separate abstract adjoint-mate identification at the
1261	  level of sheaves. Because \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism with
1262	  no flatness hypothesis whatsoever, \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is an isomorphism, hence so is
1263	  \(\alpha\) on the affine chart, and by the locality reduction so is the global
1264	  base-change map. Flatness of \(g\) plays no role in the affine lemma; it enters only
1265	  in the global statement Theorem~\ref{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}, where it is
1266	  used to commute \(- \otimes_A B\) with the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\).
1267	\end{proof}

1272	input that flatness preserves the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\). That input is
1273	supplied verbatim by Mathlib, recorded here as a dependency anchor.

1275	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
1276	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
1277	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
1278	  \mathlibok
1279	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1280	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
1281	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
1282	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
1283	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
1284	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
1285	  \[
1286	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
1287	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1288	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
1289	  \]
1290	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
1291	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
1292	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
1293	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).

1295	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
1296	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
1297	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
1298	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
1299	\end{lemma}

/-! ## The affine base-change lemma and its locality reduction -/

/-- **Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map (locality reduction).** For `f` affine
and `F` quasi-coherent, the base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism as soon
as it restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of the base `S'`. This is
the locality reduction underlying `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`: it discharges the
affine-open locality criterion `Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens` for the base-change map,
the per-affine-open hypothesis being the affine–affine section assertion supplied (over each chart)
by the section-level computation. The `[IsAffineHom f]`/`[F.IsQuasicoherent]` hypotheses are
carried because the per-open assertion `H` is only available under them (they make `X` restrict to
`Spec A` and `F` to `M^~` over each affine chart); the reduction itself is the locality criterion.
See blueprint `lem:base_change_map_affine_local`. -/
theorem base_change_map_affine_local (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
    (H : ∀ U : S'.affineOpens, IsIso ((pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F).app U)) :
    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) :=
  (Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F)).mpr H

/-- **Section-level value of the affine base-change map.** In the affine–affine model — base ring
maps `ψ : R ⟶ R'` (the base change `g = Spec ψ`) and `φ : R ⟶ A` (the affine morphism

`g' = pullback.fst`, `f' = pullback.snd` — the global-sections incarnation `Γ(α)` of the
base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism of `R'`-modules.

By the four affine dictionaries the domain `Γ(g^*(f_* M^~))` reads as `R' ⊗_R M` and the codomain
`Γ(f'_*(g')^* M^~)` reads as `(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M`, under which `Γ(α)` is the canonical cancellation
isomorphism `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (in the orientation
`Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, `r' ⊗ m ↦ (r' ⊗ 1) ⊗ m`); since `cancelBaseChange` carries no
flatness hypothesis, `Γ(α)` is an isomorphism. This is the section-level content of the affine
close: it is the per-affine-chart input to `base_change_map_affine_local` once the locality
reduction has restricted an arbitrary square to this affine–affine model. See blueprint
`lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`.

`((g')^*, (g')_*)`-unit on global sections through `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and

generic pullback square `pullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)` is the genuine crux and is the
outstanding obligation (typed `sorry` below). -/
